_note: Sprint D4 Phase C.9 example — UK 3-bed dwelling DELIBERATELY engineered with broken ring continuity to fire INV-14 FAIL HIGH on BOTH ring final circuits. Same dwelling geometry as the C.8 PASS counterpart (uk-3bed-with-ring-continuity) but with two DIFFERENT install defects: (1) C01 GF ring — endpoint_b terminated at the wrong MCB way at the consumer unit; engineer-of-record recorded the misterminated return leg at CU-MAIN-WAY-2 instead of CU-MAIN-WAY-1 — physically TWO RADIALS sharing conductors, not a real ring; the IR schema's single mcb_way_id field captures the canonical-intended way (CU-MAIN-WAY-1) and the prose evidence makes the misterminated endpoint_b explicit; continuity_verified is set to false to mirror the failed Appendix H test reading. (2) C02 1F ring — both endpoints land at the correct CU-MAIN-WAY-2 but the engineer-of-record never performed the Appendix H continuity verification at first fix (continuity_verified: false) — the install survey was incomplete and the design cannot attest that the ring was correctly closed. INV-14 FIRES HIGH on BOTH rings; compliance_summary.non_compliance_flags[] mirrors as TWO entries (one per ring) with severity=critical (the IR schema enum is {critical, warning, info} — the spec calls for HIGH severity which maps to critical given safety-critical broken ring continuity = real fault-loop hazard). compliance_summary.compliant: false. This is the FAIL counterpart to C.8 (uk-3bed-with-ring-continuity PASS) and exists to demonstrate INV-14 firing the FAIL HIGH path. The output IR is STRUCTURALLY VALID against small-power-ir.schema.json — ring_endpoints is present on both rings, all schema-required fields populated; the engineering is broken, not the schema shape. No Part-7 rooms — consumed_intents.special_locations_zoning intentionally omitted (cascade trigger does not fire).
site_brief: UK 3-bed 2-storey dwelling, ~95 m² total floor area, TN-C-S DNO supply per BS 7671:2018+A2:2022. Consumer unit CU-MAIN is a 12-way Amendment-3 board with 6 kA Icu busbar. Two ring final circuits intended (CU-MAIN-WAY-1 for GF; CU-MAIN-WAY-2 for 1F) but two install defects discovered at first fix: C01 — endpoint_b misterminated at the wrong MCB way (CU-MAIN-WAY-2 instead of CU-MAIN-WAY-1); C02 — Appendix H continuity verification never performed at first fix. One dedicated radial feeds the cooker outlet at the kitchen (32 A on 6 mm² T+E) — radial topology is unaffected. All sockets are BS 1363 13 A double switched general-purpose outlets unless noted. No bathroom shaver supply or outdoor socket on this design — the dwelling intentionally avoids Part-7 special-location rooms so the example focuses cleanly on the INV-14 FAIL HIGH demonstration. The engineer-of-record's remediation plan: (C01) reterminate endpoint_b at CU-MAIN-WAY-1 and re-test r1+r2+rn end-to-end per IET On-Site Guide Appendix H; (C02) perform the missing Appendix H continuity verification and re-issue Schedule of Test Results.

ring_continuity_endpoints._violation: endpoint_b terminated at wrong CU way — physically TWO RADIALS sharing conductors, not a real ring
Appendix H continuity verification never performed at first fix — design cannot attest the ring is closed
